passage.title: Leaf Color Chart
passage.text: Our group keeps a garden watch board next to the class garden box. In the morning, Yuna points to it before the first bell. The garden watch board has green, yellow, and brown leaf cards for the day's job. The garden helper asks us to notice what looks new in nature. Sometimes we compare today's card with yesterday's card and notice one small change. We like the garden watch board because it helps us talk about seasons clearly.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the garden watch board is used.

questions.prompt: Where is the garden watch board?
choices: next to the class garden box
questions.rationale: The first sentence says the garden watch board is next to the class garden box.

questions.prompt: When does Yuna use the garden watch board?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Yuna uses it in the morning.

questions.prompt: What does the garden watch board have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the garden watch board has leaf cards.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
